biological.physical_capacity: 5'8", lean functional build from three years of martial arts cross-training (muay thai, wrestling, jiu-jitsu). Moves economically — the kind of physical presence that registers with peers without being performed. Puberty accelerating through the summer.

attachment.forming_context: Karen is reliably present and warm, but her own anxiety transmits through her body language. Drew learns that love is dependable but slightly nervous.

active.name: emotional_inhibition
active.core_belief: Strong feelings make things worse — keep it together
active.compensatory_strategy: Channels emotion into productivity and problem-solving. Reads rooms before speaking. Defaults to 'I'm fine.' Marcus and Carol are partial exceptions — Drew is honest about thinking with them, though not about feeling. The schema is now being actively circumvented in Sofia's presence rather than managed around. The letter was still a structured medium; the courtyard conversation was live and unscripted. 'I want' came out before the filter could engage — desire moving faster than inhibition. This is not healing. The schema is intact and would reassert under stress. But something in Sofia's presence — or in what she represents — is generating enough motivational force to outrun the suppression reflex.
active.forming_context: Greg models stoic competence. Karen's anxiety spikes when Drew is upset. Plano culture rewards cheerful compliance.
active.healing_note: Age 14 — the letter was the first act of expression over suppression. The courtyard conversation went further: live, unscripted, and 'I want' escaped unfiltered. The schema is being circumvented by desire, not resolved. Genuine healing would require Drew to express vulnerability without the override of wanting — to feel uncertain and say so. That hasn't happened yet.

active.name: self_sacrifice
active.core_belief: My job is to make things easier for the people around me
active.compensatory_strategy: Volunteers for tasks, anticipates what others need, measures his own worth by usefulness. Now extending to the friendship with Marcus — Drew sometimes defers to Marcus's project ideas without examining whether he wants something different. The schema has been quiet across two significant relational moments: the Sofia letter (written for no reason except want), and lunch in the courtyard (attended for no reason except want). The Marcus conversation is the interesting case — Drew proactively protected the friendship, but not from guilt or fear of loss. He did it because he wanted both things and saw no reason they couldn't coexist. The self-sacrifice pattern wasn't driving that; genuine care was.
active.forming_context: Post-9/11 household anxiety, Greg's job instability, Karen stretched thin. Drew decided to be the kid who doesn't add to the burden.

tested_by.context: Wrote a letter to Sofia Reyes — first act of self-direction in the relational/emotional domain. Not driven by competence or usefulness, but by want. Self-direction expanded from intellectual and physical domains into desire itself. Continued into reciprocal territory: showed up to the courtyard, stayed present in an unscripted conversation, said 'I want' out loud before the inhibition could catch it. Self-direction is no longer confined to domains where Drew controls the outcome.

values.last_reordered: age 13 — self-direction moved to top as Drew's competence domains (martial arts, computers, social navigation) gave him increasing confidence in his own judgment

self_concept.agency: strong and expanding — three years of martial arts competition, real software projects, and social fluidity across groups built a robust sense of capability. Now confirmed in a domain where competence doesn't apply: Drew cannot prepare for Sofia, cannot debug a conversation mid-stream, cannot anticipate what she'll say. He showed up anyway and was present rather than managing. Agency is no longer contingent on control.
self_concept.communion: selective but genuine — deep bonds with Marcus and Carol, broad-but-shallow connections across peer groups, comfort with being between rather than belonging to. Now in active reciprocal territory for the first time: Sofia approached him, not the other way around. She said 'same time tomorrow.' Drew is discovering that communion can be pursued rather than earned, and that protecting one bond (Marcus) doesn't require sacrificing another. Communion is not zero-sum.
self_concept.dominant_sequence: emerging redemption arc — reactive excellence (ages 11-13) giving way to proactive intention. The realization that preparation without desire is just aimlessness. Two data points now: the letter and the 'I want.' The beat is laid twice, which means it's beginning to be a pattern rather than an exception.
self_concept.identity_statement: I am someone who figures things out, connects across boundaries, and prepares for what's coming. I don't need to belong to one group to belong. And I'm learning that wanting something — not because it's useful, not because someone else needs it, just because I want it — is something I can act on. The filter doesn't always win.

defenses.note: Anticipation added as a mature defense — Drew's preparation orientation (martial arts, systems thinking, social observation) is fundamentally about anticipating and preparing for challenges before they arrive. Defense profile is solidly mature under normal stress. Threshold raised after two demonstrations of resilience: the Sofia letter (tolerating vulnerability without collapsing), and the full day of unresolvable anticipation before she responded (mild-to-moderate emotional load, no regression to intellectualization as escape). Drew is getting better at sitting with uncertainty. The question is what happens when the stakes are higher — when Sofia pulls back, or when the two-world problem (Marcus and Sofia, training and connection) creates real conflict rather than clean alignment.
